## Automated Tests Required

### S28-S1: Per-floor matchup hint — Logic

**Test file path**: `tests/unit/formation_assignment/floor_picker_matchup_hint_test.gd`

**Context**: `_render_floor_picker_biome_tabs()` in
`assets/screens/formation_assignment/formation_assignment.gd` already builds
`archetype_to_class: Dictionary[String, String]` by iterating
`HeroClassDatabase.get_all_ids()` and reading `cls.counter_archetype`. The current
biome-level hint uses `biome.dominant_archetypes` as input. S28-S1 adds a per-floor hint
using `floor.enemy_list` → `EnemyDatabase.get_by_id(entry.enemy_id).archetype` as input
into the same map. The logic under test is **archetype derivation from enemy_list** — given
a floor's enemy composition, produce the correct "Recommended: ClassName" text.

**What to test**:

1. Floor with all enemies `archetype = "bruiser"` → recommended class has `counter_archetype = "bruiser"` (Warrior or Berserker)
2. Floor with all enemies `archetype = "caster"` → recommended class is Paladin (`counter_archetype = "caster"`)
3. Floor with mixed archetypes (e.g., 2 bruiser-archetype + 1 caster-archetype enemies) → dominant archetype (most frequent by enemy count) determines recommendation
4. Floor with an enemy whose `archetype` has no matching `counter_archetype` in any registered class → no recommendation label rendered (graceful absence, not crash)
5. Floor with `enemy_list = []` → no recommendation label rendered
6. Recommended class display name uses `HeroClass.display_name` (or `tr()`) — NOT a hardcoded raw English string literal

**Edge cases to cover**:

- All enemies share the same archetype → single clear recommendation, label not duplicated
- Exactly 2 archetypes tied on count → deterministic tie-breaking (first-encountered in enemy_list wins); test is repeatable
- Enemy with `archetype = ""` (empty string) → treated as no archetype, skipped from recommendation
- `EnemyDatabase.get_by_id()` returns null for an unknown `enemy_id` → graceful skip, no crash or null-dereference
- `archetype_to_class` map helper is extracted from the render method and callable from tests without booting the full scene tree

**Estimated test count**: ~6 unit tests

**Refactor note** (required for testability): Extract the `archetype_to_class` map build into a
public or protected helper — e.g.,
`_build_archetype_to_class_map() -> Dictionary[String, String]` — so the derivation logic
is testable in isolation without rendering the floor picker scene. The map-build currently
lives inside `_render_floor_picker_biome_tabs()`; moving it to a callable helper follows
the same `UIFramework.synergy_effect_text()` extraction pattern used in Sprint 27.

---

### S28-N2: Offline-unlock surfacing — Integration

**Test file path**: Extend `tests/integration/return_to_app/return_to_app_screen_test.gd`
(add a new Group C — "Offline Floor Unlock Surfacing" — or the next available group letter).

**Context**: `OfflineSummary.floors_cleared_in_window: Array[int]` already tracks floor
indices first-cleared during an offline window (line 54 of
`offline_progression_engine.gd`). `return_to_app.gd:_on_offline_rewards_collected(summary)`
calls `_render_summary(summary)`. S28-N2 adds: when
`summary.floors_cleared_in_window.size() > 0`, a floor-unlock notice label is shown
(analogous to `victory_moment.gd:_render_unlock_notice()` at line 202). Integration tests
verify the signal→render chain.

**What to test**:

1. `offline_rewards_collected` fires with `floors_cleared_in_window = [1]` → unlock notice element on `return_to_app` screen is visible; text contains the string for "Floor 2 now available" (floor_index 1 → player-facing floor number 2)
2. `offline_rewards_collected` fires with `floors_cleared_in_window = []` → unlock notice element is hidden or absent; existing gold/kills summary layout is unaffected
3. `offline_rewards_collected` fires with `floors_cleared_in_window = [1, 4]` (two floors) → notice reflects the highest new floor OR both floors, whichever the implementation chooses; test pins the chosen behavior
4. Signal fires twice (OPE re-fires after a second offline segment) → second summary's `floors_cleared_in_window` replaces the first render; notice updates correctly, no stale state

**Edge cases to cover**:

- `floors_cleared_in_window = [0]` (floor_index 0 = floor 1, always unlocked on fresh save) → unlock notice should NOT fire for floor 1 (it is never locked); ensure `floor_index >= 1` guard is enforced
- Unlock notice for a floor at a biome-unlock threshold (e.g., clearing F5 may unlock a new biome) → notice text is distinct from the biome-unlock copy used in `victory_moment.gd:_render_unlock_notice()` (line 211); locale keys must not collide

**Estimated test count**: ~4 integration tests

**Implementation reference**: See `victory_moment.gd:_render_unlock_notice()` (line 202) for
the pattern. The return_to_app unlock notice is simpler — no biome-complete path. The
existing `return_to_app.gd:_render_summary(summary)` is the correct call site.
Test isolation: use `load_save_data`/`get_save_data` snapshot-restore pattern
(see project memory `feedback_test_isolation_live_autoload`).

## Manual QA Checklist

### S28-M1 — Human-Gated: Unified Playtest 16/17/18

**Verification method**: Follow
`production/playtests/unified-playtest-16-17-18-protocol-2026-05-16.md` —
single 25-40 min session grades all 3 pending playtests simultaneously.
**Who must sign off**: Project lead (solo mode)
**Evidence to capture**: Fill in all TBD cells in `playtest-16.md`, `playtest-17.md`,
`playtest-18.md`. Mark S25/S26/S27 retros CLOSED.

- [ ] `git pull origin main` — confirm local is at c26d1bf (PR #195) or later
- [ ] Run Phases 1–7 of the unified protocol in sequence (cold launch → recruit pool → formation → dispatch → run → return-to-app → Hall review)
- [ ] All 15 per-check rows across the 3 playtest docs filled in with PASS/FAIL + notes
- [ ] Each playtest doc has a completed "Overall Verdict" section
- [ ] Sprint 25/26/27 retros updated from DRAFT to CLOSED with playtest verdict reference
- [ ] S28-M1 status updated to `done` in `sprint-status.yaml`
- [ ] Playtest backlog = 0 after this session

### S28-S1 — Logic + UI: Per-floor matchup hint

**Verification method**: Automated tests (primary) + floor picker manual walkthrough.
**Who must sign off**: Developer (test pass)
**Evidence**: Test file passing; screenshot of floor picker with "Recommended:" labels.

- [ ] `tests/unit/formation_assignment/floor_picker_matchup_hint_test.gd` exists; all ~6 tests pass via `godot --headless --script tests/gdunit4_runner.gd`
- [ ] Every floor entry in the picker that has a non-empty, matchable `enemy_list` shows a "Recommended: [ClassName]" label
- [ ] Floors with no matchable archetype (or empty `enemy_list`) show no label — not a blank "Recommended: " string
- [ ] The class name matches the expected counter class for that floor's enemies (manual spot-check: forest_reach F1 bruiser-heavy → Warrior or Berserker)
- [ ] Hint label does not overflow at 1280×800 (Steam Deck resolution)
- [ ] Existing biome-level matchup hint (if retained) still renders — no regression
- [ ] Class name in hint is not an English-hardcoded literal (`tr()` or `HeroClass.display_name`)
- [ ] `.uid` sidecar committed alongside `floor_picker_matchup_hint_test.gd`

- [ ] `production/pending-decisions.md` Prestige model row marked Resolved

- [ ] Screenshot captured in `production/qa/evidence/sprint-28-N1-vfx-textures.png` (or similar path under `production/qa/evidence/`)
- [ ] No console errors about missing texture or failed import during run
- [ ] Draw-call count stays reasonable (check Debugger → Monitors during a run; no explosion in GPU draw calls from new particle material)

- [ ] `.uid` sidecar for any new test file committed alongside it

## Smoke Test Scope

Run **after** S28-S1 and S28-N2 are implemented. Use `/smoke-check sprint` as the trigger.
Do NOT run prematurely — smoke check gates QA hand-off, not implementation start.

1. Game launches to start_menu without crash
2. Guild Hall loads → Theron in roster, gold counter visible
3. **Dispatch → floor picker opens → at least one floor entry shows "Recommended: [Class]" label** (S28-S1 regression surface)
4. Floor picker → select floor → dispatch → run resolves → Victory Moment screen
5. Victory Moment: correct unlock notice if floor was first-cleared; hidden if re-clear
6. **Return to App (simulate offline window) → if `floors_cleared_in_window` non-empty → unlock notice visible** (S28-N2 regression surface)
7. **VFX fires in combat run → no console errors about missing texture** (S28-N1 regression surface)
8. Save/load round-trip: quit → relaunch → hero roster + gold persists
9. `cat VERSION` reflects the S28-M2 catch-up value

---

## Playtest Requirements

| Story | Playtest Goal | Min Sessions | Target Player Type |
|-------|--------------|--------------|-------------------|
| S28-M1 (Unified 16/17/18) | Grade the compound S25–S27 surface: 4 classes, synergy tier ladder, effect text on Dispatch label, recruit pool = 4, boss floor visual, floor lock indicators | 1 (unified protocol covers all 3) | Project lead (solo mode) |
| S28-S1 (per-floor hint) | Does the floor-level "Recommended: ClassName" label clarify the matchup decision without being noise? Does it read at compact sizes? | 1 (foldable into S28-M1 session if S1 ships before that session runs) | Any |

**Sign-off requirement**: Playtest notes for S28-M1 must be written into
`production/playtests/playtest-16.md`, `playtest-17.md`, and `playtest-18.md`
(each has a Per-Check Validation table with TBD cells). If S28-S1 ships before the
unified playtest session, Phase 2 of the unified protocol (floor picker interaction)
naturally covers it — no separate session required.

---

## Definition of Done — Sprint 28

A story is DONE when ALL of the following are true:

- [ ] All acceptance criteria verified via automated test result OR documented manual evidence (screenshot, playtest notes with sign-off)
- [ ] **S28-S1**: `tests/unit/formation_assignment/floor_picker_matchup_hint_test.gd` exists; ~6 tests pass
- [ ] **S28-N2**: `tests/integration/return_to_app/return_to_app_screen_test.gd` extended with unlock-surfacing group; passes
- [ ] Manual evidence exists for S28-N1 (screenshot) and S28-M1 (filled-in playtest docs)
- [ ] Smoke check passes (`/smoke-check sprint` — run after S28-S1 + S28-N2 land, not before)
- [ ] No regressions: floor picker biome hint still renders; return_to_app gold/kills summary unaffected; save/load round-trip intact
- [ ] Code reviewed for each implementation PR
- [ ] Story statuses updated to `done` in `production/sprint-status.yaml` as each completes
- [ ] **Content PRs (S1/N1/N2) only land after S28-M1 playtest verdict = PASS** (sprint process rule — unlock gate)
